rulings.Case Title: STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA A PUBLIC CORPORATION VS. LEONARD R MILSTEIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Mtn To Dismiss; Mtn To Dismiss; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 11, 2012, Line 2. DEFENDANT LEONARD MILSTEIN Ntc Of Hearing On Mtn To Dismiss; Mtn To Dismiss; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos. Denied. Defendant's repeated requests for continuances, on the grounds of preserving his Fifth Amendment rights in the criminal proceedings, effectively stayed the civil proceedings, thereby making it impractical for Plaintiff to bring the action to trial during the time Defendant made it clear that he opposed this action going forward so as to preserve his Fifth Amendment rights. By his words and conduct, Defendant waived, and is estopped from asserting, his right to seek a dismissal under the five year statute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. rulings.Case Title: MARK ROBINSON III VS. SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 11, 2012, Line 6. DEFENDANT SIRRON NORRIS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Denied. Mr. Norris has not satisfied his summary judgment burden. Mr. Norris has not shown that his telephone calls to PVCC are protected by Civil Code 47(b) because there is no evidence that PVCC's job selection process was an "official proceeding." Mr. Norris has not shows that Civil Code 47(c) applies because there is no evidence that he is an interested person for purposes of that qualified privilege. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARK ROBINSON III VS. SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 11, 2012, Line 6. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT, DON GRIFFIN, LAURIE SCOLARI MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment by the District and Ms. Scolari are granted. The motion is moot as to defendant Griffin and the second and third causes of action for interference because plaintiff dismissed Mr. Griffin and those claims. The District and Ms. Scolari satisfied their burden of production and plaintiff did not present any evidence creating a triable issue as to the first cause of action for defamation, fourth cause of action for invasion of privacy and fifth cause of action for retaliation. As to the first and fourth claims, there is no evidence that Ms. Scolari, Mr. Griffin or the District published a defamatory statement or disclosed a private fact about plaintiff. Asa to the fifth claim, the only cognizable adverse employment action was the Notice of Disciplinary Action and alleged constructive termination. The other cited incidents of retaliation are not actionable and/or untimely because the earliest protected activity cited in the complaint was the filing of the government claim. Mr. Griffin explains that he issued the Notice of Disciplinary Action in response to the second Oppenheimer investigation. Plaintiff has not presented sufficient responsive evidence showing that Mr. Griffin's reasons were pretextual or that there is a nexus between the protected activity and the adverse employment action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EDISON TUMAZI VS. ROGELIO VELASQUEZ GARCIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 11, 2012, Line 7. DEFENDANT HEATH CERAMICS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Granted. Defendant Heath Ceramics is not liable as a matter of law for the alleged tort of Defendant Rogelio Velazquez Garcia. Employers are not liable for an employee's torts while the employee is going to or coming from work, and Plaintiff has not presented any evidence creating a triable issue as to any exception to the going and coming rule. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LINSEY KRAMER VS. SPLASH TRANSPORTATION, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 11, 2012, Line 8. DEFENDANT DR. PEPPER SNAPPLE GROUP, INC. A CORPORATION, THE AMERICAN BOTTLING COMPANY, SPLASH TRANSPORT, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Granted. The second cause of action is not a distinct claim from the first cause of action because the sole basis that moving defendants can be liable is due to any negligence committed by Mr. Hurtado. Defendants' binding admission of vicarious liability for any negligence of Mr. Hurtado makes it unnecessary for any determination to be made as to plaintiff's theories of negligent supervision, training and entrustment. No permissible purpose is served by retaining the second cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
